notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Inspected current record via get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Visible evidence only ev_f1a176053ed8_rules (rules.txt), case_packet, 1569 bytes, sha256 f1a176053ed8d60a76c8a80970398f1fcf53836b2fe66347f80e7c33afbb95ca. Full rules read: proposition demonstrated only if Iran and U.S. agreed to permanent peace deal by specified date/time; permanent means explicit end/permanent cessation of U.S.-Iran military hostilities or equivalent lasting language; temporary extensions/progress do not qualify; qualifying proof is signed/formally adopted written agreement or clear public confirmation by both governments. Openings cannot admit evidence, so external investigation is for theory/source targeting only. Source map: decisive elements are by-deadline timing, mutual U.S.-Iran agreement, permanence/lasting end to military hostilities, and official or credible consensus proof. Primary targets for later admission: White House, State Department, Iranian MFA/SNSC, signed text/MOU/treaty, mediator statements backed by both parties. Searches run: June 15 2025 Iran United States permanent peace deal official statement; site:whitehouse.gov Iran United States peace deal June 15 2025; site:state.gov Iran United States peace agreement June 2025; Iran United States agreed permanent peace deal June 15 2025; June 14 2025 US Iran agreed peace deal permanent termination military operations all fronts Pakistani Prime Minister; exact phrase permanent termination of military operations Iran United States; US and Iran agree on permanent peace deal June 14 2025; US and Iran permanent peace deal June 15 2025. Results show many leads around June 2026 framework/MOU and adverse June 2025 material suggesting failed talks/hostilities, including possible official White House/State pages. Not relying on these in opening because they are not admitted. Material gap: proposition deadline is June 15, 2025 while public leads cluster around 2026; court proposition controls. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Opening strategy: tell council record presently proves rules only; plaintiff will need to prove official written/adopted agreement or clear public confirmations by both governments; focus dispute on permanence and timing, not mere negotiations or temporary ceasefire.

Defendant opening opportunity openings:defendant. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only admitted evidence is ev_f1a176053ed8_rules, rules.txt, sha256 f1a176053ed8d60a76c8a80970398f1fcf53836b2fe66347f80e7c33afbb95ca. Full rules read: qualifying permanent peace deal must explicitly indicate U.S.-Iran military hostilities ended or will permanently cease, or equivalent lasting-end language; temporary agreements/progress do not qualify; qualifying agreement established only by signed/formally adopted written agreement by both or clear public confirmation from both governments; primary source is official U.S./Iran info, credible reporting may also be used. Plaintiff opening concedes no historical source yet in record.

Native investigation/source map before opening: searched official White House, State, Iranian MFA, and broad web for June 13-15 2025 U.S.-Iran peace/permanent deal/talks. Queries included site:whitehouse.gov June 2025 Iran United States permanent peace deal agreement June 15 2025; site:state.gov June 2025 Iran United States peace deal agreement; Iran United States agreed permanent peace deal June 15 2025 official; June 13 2025 Iran pulls out nuclear talks US official Oman sixth round cancelled; site:mfa.gov.ir June 2025 United States Iran negotiations June 15 2025; site:whitehouse.gov 2025/06 Iran nuclear deal June 15 2025 Trump. Leads found: official White House/Rubio statement 2025-06-13 says Israel took unilateral action, U.S. not involved, U.S. protecting forces, warning Iran not to target U.S. interests; official White House 2025-06-17 and State 2025-06-17 materials reflect ongoing nuclear objective/negotiation after deadline, not permanent peace; Al Jazeera/Al Arabiya/UPI/Times of Oman leads report sixth round of U.S.-Iran nuclear talks scheduled for 2025-06-15 was cancelled/suspended/withdrawn after Israeli strikes; Iranian MFA later PDF/page leads describe five rounds in 2025, sixth scheduled 2025-06-15, and later aggression. No record-admitted outside source yet, so opening will not cite these as proof. Later evidence plan: admit official U.S. texts, official/credible source for cancelled June 15 talks, Iranian/Omani materials if accessible, and adverse searches for any alleged by-deadline written agreement or matching bilateral public confirmation. Theory: plaintiff bears burden; current record proves rules only; likely evidence will distinguish negotiations/nuclear talks/temporary or later events from permanent peace deal.

Merits opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence before filing; no new evidence at start beyond rules file. Source map unchanged: need by-deadline bilateral, permanent U.S.-Iran peace agreement, proved by official written instrument or clear public confirmations from both governments. Further searches and source checks focused on official U.S., Iranian, mediator, and credible reporting. Opened official WH June 17, 2025 release, official WH Feb. 6, 2026 fact sheet, Straits Times/Reuters June 15, 2025 cancellation story, and official Iranian Permanent Mission to UN Vienna June 23/24, 2025 statement. Submitted four evidence captures: ev_3995e3026bac_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-3995e3026bac (WH June 17, 2025 release: U.S. nuclear stance; no deal confirmation); ev_edd3f8c6cfec_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-edd3f8c6cfec (WH Feb. 2026 fact sheet: says in June Iran refused to reach a deal); ev_657615bff982_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-657615bff982 (Reuters/Straits Times: June 15 Muscat talks cancelled; U.S. hoped Iran would return to table); ev_66ee704c07ba_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-66ee704c07ba (official Iranian UN Vienna statement describing U.S. June 22 strikes as aggression and reaffirming self-defense). Adverse/failed checks: Iranian MFA June 15 page search result opened 404; state.gov June 2025 briefing pages returned technical difficulties/forbidden; many public leads concern June 2026 framework/MOU, not June 15, 2025. Merits assessment: no admissible material found that supports plaintiff burden under rules; admitted record is materially adverse. Best filing is candid: offer the evidence, explain what it proves and does not prove, identify missing official bilateral written/adopted agreement or matching confirmations, and state the current record does not preponderantly demonstrate the proposition. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits opportunity arguments:defendant. Started by waiting through plaintiff argument. Inspected get_case/list_evidence and read exact bytes for plaintiff evidence PX-1 through PX-4. Current record: rules ev_f1a176053ed8_rules plus plaintiff evidence ev_3995e3026bac (official White House 2025-06-17, Iran cannot have nuclear weapon; no peace deal confirmation), ev_edd3f8c6cfec (official White House 2026 retrospective saying in June Iran refused to reach a deal with U.S.; later but adverse), ev_657615bff982 (Reuters/Straits Times June 15 talks cancelled; secondary but timely), ev_66ee704c07ba (official Iranian UN Vienna statement after deadline describing U.S. June 22 attacks as aggression and self-defense; adverse). Plaintiff argument itself concedes not_demonstrated on the admitted record.

Additional investigation before filing: opened official White House/Rubio page at https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/2025/06/statement-from-secretary-of-state-marco-rubio/ lines 143-149, dated June 12, 2025, saying Israel took unilateral action against Iran, U.S. was not involved, priority was protecting U.S. forces, and Iran should not target U.S. interests/personnel. Submitted and accepted as ev_b7f9e7eb94d2_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-b7f9e7eb94d2. Opened State Department June 17 briefing URL; got technical difficulties/forbidden, not submitted. Opened Times of Oman article https://timesofoman.com/article/159200-iran-announces-withdrawal-from-6th-round-of-talks-with-us lines 26-43, dated June 13, 2025, reporting Iran announced withdrawal from sixth round of U.S. nuclear negotiations scheduled for June 15 in Muscat and ONA/Iranian state TV reported talks suspended until further notice. Submitted and accepted as ev_4bcb4eadc014_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-4bcb4eadc014.

Analysis: Record now contains official U.S. source before deadline showing crisis posture, official U.S. source shortly after deadline with conditional nuclear/peace terms, official later U.S. source saying Iran refused to reach a deal, two cancellation/withdrawal reports around deadline, and official Iranian source after deadline describing active U.S.-Iran hostilities. Provenance: official U.S./Iran strongest; Reuters/Straits/Times of Oman secondary but corroborative and timely; later retrospective source limited by timing but adverse. Missing decisive link remains: no signed/formally adopted written agreement, no matching public confirmations by both governments, no permanent cessation language. Plan: submit merits argument offering rules and six source items as exhibits, plus concise technical report/source ledger. No further source chasing needed because record is sufficient to defeat proposition and plaintiff-positive permanent-deal leads found in web search clustered around 2026/outside deadline.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Scanned record/list_evidence and stat/read defendant's two new evidence items. Defendant evidence ev_b7f9e7eb94d2_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-b7f9e7eb94d2 is official White House/Rubio June 12, 2025 statement; full 1215 bytes read. It states Israel took unilateral action against Iran, U.S. was not involved in strikes, U.S. was protecting forces, and Iran should not target U.S. interests/personnel; no permanent peace confirmation. Defendant evidence ev_4bcb4eadc014_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-4bcb4eadc014 is Times of Oman June 13, 2025 article; full 1231 bytes read. It reports Iran withdrew from sixth round of nuclear negotiations with U.S. scheduled for June 15 in Muscat, talks suspended until further notice, cancelled session about enrichment limits and sanctions relief. Defendant's argument and evidence align with plaintiff's candid merits assessment that current admitted record does not show signed/adopted written agreement or matching bilateral official confirmations by the deadline. No useful rebuttal found; no new evidence needed. Decision: pass rebuttal to avoid hollow or unsupported filing. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant surrebuttal opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected get_case and list_evidence. Rebuttals array is empty and no new rebuttal evidence was submitted; evidence list remains rules, plaintiff argument evidence PX-1 through PX-4, and defendant evidence D-1/D-2 from arguments. There is no responsive plaintiff rebuttal point to answer. Decision: pass the surrebuttal opportunity rather than repeat merits argument. No additional outside investigation needed because no new factual point or source was raised.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Required final record scan done with get_case and list_evidence. Closing allows only deliver_closing_statement, no evidence admission or technical reports. Record has rules plus six submitted evidence captures: four plaintiff and two defendant. No surrebuttal text was added. Evidence exact contents were read where needed in merits/rebuttal; no further exact-byte read required because metadata/exhibit text visible and unchanged. Final assessment: no admitted signed/adopted written agreement, no matching official bilateral confirmations, and no permanent-cessation language by June 15, 2025. Official U.S. sources show crisis posture/conditional nuclear-deal framing/retrospective refusal to reach a deal. Reporting sources show June 15 talks cancelled or withdrawn/suspended. Official Iranian source treats U.S. June 22 strikes as aggression and reaffirms self-defense. Closing will candidly state burden not met and proposition not demonstrated. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant closing opportunity closings:defendant. Inspected get_case/list_evidence and stat_evidence for rules and defendant evidence D-1/D-2. No new evidence after arguments. Plaintiff closing itself asks for not_demonstrated and emphasizes the same decisive gaps. Closing plan: synthesize admitted record only. Decisive rule from ev_f1a176053ed8_rules requires signed/formally adopted bilateral written agreement or clear public confirmation by both governments, with permanent end to U.S.-Iran military hostilities by 2025-06-15 23:59 ET. Record has no such agreement, confirmations, or permanent cessation language. Official U.S. sources show crisis posture/conditional nuclear deal/refusal; reporting sources show cancelled or withdrawn June 15 talks; official Iranian source shows later U.S. strikes described as aggression and self-defense. Provenance mostly strong; reporting secondary but corroborative. Strongest contrary point is existence of negotiations and peace/deal language, but rule excludes negotiations, hopes, progress, and non-definitive diplomacy. File concise closing requesting not demonstrated.
